skill.name: firecrawl-download
skill.description: Download a website or documentation section into organized local files using the Firecrawl CLI.
skill.summary: Save websites as local markdown, link, and screenshot files with Firecrawl.
skill.author: firecrawl

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found command execution, network URLs, filesystem traversal strings, and weak-crypto patterns. Review confirms the real risks are expected Firecrawl CLI execution, network scraping, and local file creation; the path traversal and weak-crypto findings are false positives from Markdown links and text patterns. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, obfuscation, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution for Website Downloads
medium_findings.description: The skill grants Bash access to firecrawl and npx firecrawl, then instructs agents to run download commands. This is expected for the skill, but it can execute installed CLI code and should be limited to trusted Firecrawl usage.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools block and command examples directly require external command execution. The commands match the skill purpose, so the concern is operational risk rather than confirmed abuse.

medium_findings.title: Network Scraping and Local File Creation
medium_findings.description: The documented workflow maps and scrapes websites, then saves content under .firecrawl. This can download large or sensitive content if the target URL and include or exclude filters are not reviewed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly states that it maps sites, scrapes pages, and writes nested directories. This behavior is legitimate for the skill but creates network and filesystem exposure.

low_findings.title: Static Path Traversal Findings Are Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The ../ strings appear only in Markdown links to neighboring Firecrawl skills. No evidence found that these paths are used for file reads, writes, or command arguments.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The path traversal pattern appears inside Markdown links in a See also section. There is no executable code using these relative paths.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain skill metadata and an options table, not cryptographic code. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, encryption, hashing, or signature verification logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of both flagged lines shows prose and table formatting only. The skill contains no cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Example Targets
low_findings.description: The static network findings point to docs.example.com in command examples. These URLs demonstrate Firecrawl usage and are not hidden endpoints or exfiltration destinations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs use the example.com reserved domain pattern inside a Quick start section. They are not paired with secrets or unexpected request logic.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Tool Access to Firecrawl Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill allows Bash execution for firecrawl and npx firecrawl commands. Users should confirm the target site and output scope before running downloads.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration is direct evidence of command execution access. The allowed command scope is narrow but still executes external CLI behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Automated Confirmation Bypass
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill tells agents to always pass -y, which skips Firecrawl confirmation prompts. This improves automation but increases the chance of unintended downloads.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The instruction to skip confirmation is explicit. It is not malicious, but it reduces an important user review step for network and filesystem operations.

content.value_statement: Saving web documentation for offline use can require many manual page exports. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Firecrawl downloads with formats, filters, and screenshots.
actual_capabilities: Runs Firecrawl download commands for a target website.
Maps a site before scraping discovered pages.
Saves results in organized local directories under .firecrawl.
Supports markdown, links, screenshots, and multiple output formats.
Filters downloads with include paths, exclude paths, search, and limits.
Passes standard Firecrawl scrape options through the download workflow.
limitations: Requires the Firecrawl CLI or npx access to be available.
Depends on network access and the target site's crawl permissions.
Can create many local files if limits and filters are too broad.
Does not verify copyright, access rights, or data retention policies.

use_cases.title: Archive Product Documentation
use_cases.description: Download selected documentation sections as markdown and screenshots for offline review.

use_cases.title: Collect Research Sources
use_cases.description: Save a bounded website section into local files for later reading and citation checks.

use_cases.title: Prepare Migration Inputs
use_cases.description: Export pages, links, and page screenshots before a content migration or site audit.

prompt_templates.title: Download a Small Site
prompt_templates.prompt: Download this documentation site with Firecrawl as markdown. Limit the run to 20 pages and save the local files for offline reading.

prompt_templates.title: Capture Pages with Screenshots
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Firecrawl to download this site section with markdown and screenshots. Keep the page limit small and confirm the output location.

prompt_templates.title: Filter Documentation Sections
prompt_templates.prompt: Download only the features and SDK pages from this documentation site. Exclude translated pages and save markdown plus link files.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Controlled Archive
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Firecrawl download command for this site with include paths, exclude paths, page limits, screenshots, and main-content extraction. Explain the expected local outputs before running it.

output_examples.input: Download a documentation site for offline reading.
output_examples.output: The assistant prepares a Firecrawl download command, limits the page count, and saves markdown files in a local .firecrawl directory.

output_examples.input: Save selected product pages with screenshots.
output_examples.output: The assistant filters the requested paths, enables screenshot capture, and reports where the saved page files were placed.
The assistant uses include and exclude path filters to collect SDK pages while skipping translated sections.

best_practices: Set a page limit before running broad downloads.
Use include and exclude paths to keep the archive focused.
Review the target site's access rules before scraping content.
anti_patterns: Do not run full-site downloads without a clear scope.
Do not skip reviewing output size when screenshots are enabled.
Do not download private or restricted content without permission.

faq.question: What does this skill download?
faq.answer: It downloads website pages discovered by Firecrawl and saves selected formats as local files.

faq.question: Can it save screenshots?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents screenshot and full-page screenshot options from Firecrawl.

faq.question: Where are files saved?
faq.answer: The skill states that files are organized under a local .firecrawl directory.

faq.question: Can I limit which pages are downloaded?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports page limits, search filters, include paths, and exclude paths.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: Yes. Firecrawl must reach the target website to map and scrape pages.

faq.question: Is this safe to use on private sites?
faq.answer: Use caution. Confirm authorization, scope, and storage rules before downloading private or restricted content.
